Man Utd make contact with Paulo Dybala over £85m transfer as Juventus look to sell striker

claims Juventus are ready to sell the Argentine, 25, this summer after falling short in the Champions League.
And United are ready to land Dybala ahead of Bayern Munich after their place in next season’s Europa League was confirmed on Sunday.

The Red Devils are in need of a huge rebuild under Ole Gunnar Solskjaer following their disappointing 1-1 draw at relegated Huddersfield.

And SunSport understands Dybala is a top transfer target with Romelu Lukaku’s Old Trafford future uncertain.

The former Palermo ace has scored just five times in Serie A this season.

And Juve are ready to replace him with Benfica prodigy Joao Felix, with £85m said to be enough to secure Dybala's signature.

However, Inter Milan are reportedly set to offer wantaway striker Mauro Icardi in a swap deal.

While Juve are even considering using Dybala as a pawn to land Mo Salah in a shock agreement with Liverpool.
